message:
fcp-graphql: collision-free dedup keys (br-upp69)

Previously the in-flight-request dedup map keyed on a u64 DefaultHasher
(SipHash) output. Two distinct bodies hashing to the same u64 would be
treated as the same in-flight entry, so the second caller would
receive the response intended for the first. Random-input collisions
are birthday-bound at ~2^32 entries (not a practical risk), but
adversarial collisions only need knowledge of the hasher seed and
DefaultHasher is not HashDoS-resistant.

Fix: key the dedup map on the raw request body bytes (`Vec<u8>`)
instead of a 64-bit hash. Byte-for-byte equality is
collision-free unconditionally, so distinct requests always occupy
distinct map entries.

Implementation:
- HashMap<u64, SharedRequestFuture> → HashMap<Vec<u8>, SharedRequestFuture>.
- DedupGuard.key: u64 → Vec<u8>.
- Drop::drop uses std::mem::take(&mut self.key) for the contended
  spawn-fallback branch since Vec<u8> isn't Copy.
- Call site moves body_bytes into the guard and clones it for the
  map insert; the payload clone into the future's closure is
  unchanged.
- hash_bytes() and its 7 tests removed; DedupGuard tests rewritten
  to use byte-slice keys.

Memory cost: one extra Vec<u8> allocation per in-flight dedup entry,
bounded by concurrency and released by DedupGuard::drop.

Regression test: dedup_map_distinguishes_bodies_that_would_share_siphash_output
proves two bodies differing in a single byte occupy two distinct map
entries and resolve to their respective responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
